name: Don Abney
abstract: John Donald Abney (March 10, 1923 – January 27, 2000) was an American jazz pianist.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Jazz at the Philharmonic
Midnight Performance: Jazz at the Philharmonic
Concert In Jazz: A Program of Modern American Music

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: when I came up, there was him and Don Abney -- he's another one... That's another school of piano playing that I was exposed to.

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: later on we had a little band with Zoot Sims, George Duvivier on bass, Charlie Shavers, and Don Abney on piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Lamb
about_quotes.evidence: And Don Abney who was the piano player, and all these guys were well seasoned.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Lamb interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Don Abney only played the dinner hour. He played up till 9 o'clock by himself. I had the trio.
